title: Type Definitions
content:
This file defines the core type structures used throughout the DocMed application.

## Key Exports

*   **`ManifestEntry`**: Represents an entry in the documentation manifest, mapping a file path to its human-readable title and a unique `docKey` for referencing.
    *   `filePath`: The path to the documentation file.
    *   `title`: The display title for the document.
    *   `docKey`: A unique identifier for the document.

*   **`DocFile`**: Represents a loaded documentation file, including its path, title, and the actual content.
    *   `filePath`: The path to the documentation file.
    *   `title`: The display title for the document.
    *   `content`: The raw content of the documentation file.

*   **`Commit`**: Represents information about a git commit.
    *   `id`: The SHA-1 hash of the commit.
    *   `message`: The commit message.
    *   `pusher`: The name of the user who pushed the commit.
    *   `timestamp`: The timestamp of the commit.

*   **`View`**: A discriminated union representing the different views or states the application can be in.
    *   `{ type: "home" }`: Represents the home view of the application.
    *   `{ type: "commits" }`: Represents the view displaying commit history.
    *   `{ type: "doc"; key: string }`: Represents a view displaying a specific documentation document, identified by its `key`.
    *   `{ type: "folder"; path: string }`: Represents a view displaying the contents of a specific folder, identified by its `path`.

*   **`Tab`**: Represents a tab in the UI, linking a specific view to a user-facing label.
    *   `id`: A unique identifier for the tab.
    *   `view`: The `View` configuration associated with this tab.
    *   `label`: The text displayed on the tab.

## Usage Example

These types are fundamental for state management and data flow within DocMed. For instance, when fetching and displaying a document, you might use `DocFile` and `View` like so:

```typescript
// Assuming 'documentData' is of type DocFile and 'currentView' is of type View

function displayDocument(documentData: DocFile, currentView: View) {
  if (currentView.type === "doc") {
    console.log(`Displaying document: ${documentData.title}`);
    console.log(`Content: ${documentData.content}`);
  }
}
```
